summary: Iteration 33 validated MongoDB persistence of agent check-ins, PRIOR_INSIGHTS prompt enrichment of /me/agent/chat, and the manual AI Companion header button. Backend: 5/5 pytest tests pass in /app/backend/tests/test_agent_checkin.py — 401 unauthenticated, 400 unknown suggestion kind, 200 happy-path with direct MongoDB read-back (user_id, message, suggestion, session_id, created_at all persisted in agent_checkins collection), PRIOR_INSIGHTS injection confirmed (after seeding 'anxious about meeting → 396 Hz preset', a subsequent /me/agent/chat with similar mood returns a message referencing '396'/'last time'), and 50-row per-user trim (insert 52, count_documents==50). Frontend (admin Pro login on https://frequency-healer-31.preview.emergentagent.com): AIAgentSheet still auto-opens once per session with 'Hello Admin, how are you feeling right now?'; the new data-testid='ai-companion-button' renders next to data-testid='account-button' in the left rail (Sparkles icon, gold #C4A67A, title='AI Companion'); clicking it opens the sheet with exactly 'How can I help you?' as the first agent-assistant-msg; sending a message returns suggestions; tapping a suggestion fires POST /api/me/agent/checkin (confirmed via network capture: payload includes mood message + chosen suggestion + session_id); sheet auto-closes after applySuggestion; reload does NOT re-trigger the auto-open (sessionStorage 'sf_agent_seen_v1' gate); manual button still works after reload. Mobile 390x844 viewport: AI Companion button visible and clickable; sheet opens with correct greeting.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py /me/agent/checkin (L1158-1215): clean Pydantic validation + bounded re-serialization of the suggestion dict before persistence — no raw client-shaped data lands in Mongo. Defensive trim block uses sort+skip+delete_many and is wrapped in try/except so housekeeping failures never bubble up to the user. Good.

server.py /me/agent/chat (L1099-1118) caps prior_insights at 3 rows + 60-char labels, keeping the LLM prompt token budget bounded. The prompt explicitly says MAY reference (not MUST), so flakiness in callback wording is by design — the test asserts presence of any of 7 signal tokens to absorb that variance.

MINOR — Test parallelism: the new test_agent_checkin.py shares a clean_checkins fixture that wipes by user_id. When pytest-xdist runs the prior-insights test on one worker and the trim test on another (both touching the admin's agent_checkins collection), the wipes race and the PRIOR_INSIGHTS test can fail intermittently. Running sequentially (`-o addopts=`) makes all 5 deterministic. Consider gating these tests with `@pytest.mark.xdist_group(name="agent_checkins")` or making each test use a unique user (e.g. a freshly registered TEST_ user) so workers don't collide on the shared admin account. Not blocking — sequential pass is 5/5.

Dashboard.jsx openCompanion (L306-309) does setAgentGreeting('How can I help you?') + setAgentOpen(true) in the same callback; React 18 batches the two state updates so the AIAgentSheet effect [open, greeting] re-runs with the new greeting on remount. Live-verified — greeting flips correctly between auto-open ('Hello Admin…') and manual-open ('How can I help you?').

AIAgentSheet.jsx applySuggestion fires POST /me/agent/checkin fire-and-forget (no await on the user's behalf, no blocking UX). Verified end-to-end: payload body includes the user's mood message + the suggestion dict + a session_id; the persisted row is visible to the next /me/agent/chat call as PRIOR_INSIGHTS within the same session.
seed_data_creation: test_agent_checkin.py uses a clean_checkins fixture that wipes/restores agent_checkins for the admin user before+after each test. No long-lived seed data leaks; trim test deliberately inserts 52 rows then trim → 50.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend persistence collection name is `agent_checkins` (NOT agent_checkin). To read it directly use motor with MONGO_URL+DB_NAME from /app/backend/.env. To run the new test file deterministically use: `pytest backend/tests/test_agent_checkin.py -v -o addopts=` (suppresses the default pytest-xdist parallelism declared in /app/backend/pytest.ini — required because two tests in the file both wipe the admin's agent_checkins collection and otherwise race). UI: the AI Companion sheet exposes data-testid='ai-agent-sheet' (whole modal), 'ai-agent-close' (X button), 'ai-agent-messages' (scroll region), 'agent-assistant-msg' / 'agent-user-msg' (bubbles), 'agent-suggestions' (the row), 'agent-suggestion-<kind>-<idx>' (each tappable card). Network: every applySuggestion tap fires POST /api/me/agent/checkin in the background (fire-and-forget) — easy to verify via page.on('request',…). sessionStorage gate key is still 'sf_agent_seen_v1'='1' for the auto-open suppression.

test_results_detail.BE_checkin_persists_to_mongo: PASS — direct motor read confirms user_id, message, suggestion(preset/396), session_id, created_at all stored
test_results_detail.BE_prior_insights_referenced_in_next_cha: PASS — LLM reply contained '396' / 'last time' token after seeding (anxious → 396 Hz preset)
test_results_detail.FE_T1_auto_open_personalised_greeting: PASS — 'Hello Admin, how are you feeling right now?'
test_results_detail.FE_T2_companion_button_attrs: PASS — title='AI Companion', class contains '#C4A67A', 1 SVG (Sparkles)
test_results_detail.FE_T3_account_button_adjacent: PASS — both buttons present in left rail header
test_results_detail.FE_T4_manual_open_greeting_exact: PASS (verified on second reproduction; first reproduction had a 500ms-wait timing flake that resolved on 1000ms wait — the production UX is correct, the sheet does seed 'How can I help you?' on every manual open)
test_results_detail.FE_T5_suggestion_fires_checkin: PASS — POST /api/me/agent/checkin observed with full payload {message, suggestion:{kind:'preset', label:'432 Hz · Deep Calm', frequency:432, waveform:'sine', pro_only:false}, session_id}
